event: Neolithic Linear Pottery culture farmers settle the loess soils of southern Limburg, bringing the first agriculture to the region.
The Funnelbeaker culture builds the hunebedden, large megalithic dolmen tombs concentrated in Drenthe.
Julius Caesar campaigns against the Belgic and Germanic tribes of the Low Countries during the Gallic Wars.
Roman general Drusus campaigns along the Rhine, which becomes the northern frontier of the Roman Empire.
The Batavian leader Gaius Julius Civilis leads a major revolt against Roman rule along the Rhine.
Frankish and Saxon raids intensify as Roman control over the lower Rhine frontier weakens.
Roman authority collapses in the region as the frontier is abandoned during the great migrations.
The Anglo-Saxon missionary Willibrord begins Christianizing the Frisians as Archbishop of the Frisians.
The missionary Boniface is martyred at Dokkum while evangelizing the Frisians.
Charlemagne subdues Frisia and the surrounding lands, incorporating them into the Frankish Empire.
The Treaty of Verdun divides the Carolingian Empire, placing the Low Countries largely within Middle Francia.
The St. Lucia's flood drowns much of the north, helping to form the Zuiderzee inland sea.
Philip the Bold of Burgundy marries into Flanders, beginning the Burgundian unification of the Low Countries.
Philip the Good of Burgundy gains control of Holland, Zeeland, and Hainaut, consolidating Burgundian rule.
Mary of Burgundy marries Maximilian of Habsburg, bringing the Low Countries under Habsburg control.
The Dutch Revolt against Spanish rule erupts, beginning the Eighty Years' War.
The Sea Beggars capture Brielle, igniting widespread rebellion in Holland and Zeeland.
The Union of Utrecht unites the northern provinces, laying the foundation of the Dutch Republic.
The Act of Abjuration formally declares independence from King Philip II of Spain.
The Dutch East India Company is founded, becoming the world's first multinational chartered trading company.
The Bank of Amsterdam is established and a truce with Spain begins, opening the Dutch Golden Age.
The Peace of Westphalia ends the Eighty Years' War, formally recognizing Dutch independence.
The First Anglo-Dutch War breaks out over maritime trade and naval supremacy.
The 'Disaster Year' sees France, England, and others invade as William III rises to power as stadtholder.
Stadtholder William III invades England in the Glorious Revolution, becoming king of England the next year.
French revolutionary forces help establish the Batavian Republic, ending the old Dutch Republic.
Napoleon creates the Kingdom of Holland with his brother Louis Bonaparte as king.
The Congress of Vienna creates the United Kingdom of the Netherlands under King William I.
The Belgian Revolution leads to the secession of the southern provinces as Belgium.
A liberal constitution drafted by Thorbecke establishes parliamentary government under a constitutional monarchy.
The Netherlands abolishes slavery in its colonies, including Suriname and the Caribbean.
The Netherlands declares neutrality and remains neutral throughout World War I.
Universal male suffrage and proportional representation are introduced, with women's suffrage following in 1919.
The Afsluitdijk barrier dam is completed, closing off the Zuiderzee to create the freshwater IJsselmeer.
Nazi Germany invades and occupies the Netherlands, bombing Rotterdam, while the royal family flees to Britain.
Operation Market Garden fails at Arnhem, and the brutal 'Hunger Winter' famine grips the occupied west.
The Netherlands is liberated from German occupation as World War II ends in Europe.
The Netherlands recognizes the independence of Indonesia after a four-year war of decolonization.
The Netherlands becomes a founding member of NATO.
The catastrophic North Sea flood kills over 1,800 people, prompting the Delta Works flood defenses.
The Netherlands becomes a founding member of the European Economic Community via the Treaty of Rome.
Suriname gains independence from the Netherlands.
Queen Beatrix accedes to the throne after the abdication of her mother Queen Juliana.
The Maastricht Treaty, signed in the Dutch city of Maastricht, establishes the European Union.
The Netherlands becomes the first country in the world to legalize same-sex marriage.
The euro replaces the guilder as the national currency.
King Willem-Alexander accedes to the throne after Queen Beatrix abdicates, the first Dutch king since 1890.
Long-serving prime minister Mark Rutte's coalition collapses over migration policy, ending an era of Dutch politics.
